title: Another earthquake another setback
wikitext:
[]: Another earthquake, another setback? Post by Merkaba » Fri Oct 25, 2013 1:51 pm http://rt.com/news/japan-earthquake-tsunami-honshu-748/ Any guesses on how far this will set 4.0 back?

[]: Post by Dataprime » Fri Oct 25, 2013 1:58 pm I think they are more impotent issues regarding a earthquake (i.e loss of human life) than a movie getting delayed.

[]: Post by Princess Asuka » Fri Oct 25, 2013 2:07 pm Dataprime wrote: I think they are more impotent issues regarding a earthquake (i.e loss of human life) than a movie getting delayed.

[]: Post by Ænimal » Fri Oct 25, 2013 2:19 pm Dataprime wrote: I think they are more impotent issues regarding a earthquake (i.e loss of human life) than a movie getting delayed.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Fri Oct 25, 2013 2:24 pm Woha, 7.3? They never seem to get a break...

[]: Post by Merkaba » Fri Oct 25, 2013 3:38 pm Dataprime wrote: I think they are more impotent issues regarding a earthquake (i.e loss of human life) than a movie getting delayed.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Fri Oct 25, 2013 3:45 pm Merkaba wrote: I didn't join this forum to feign pity over the loss of human life. I joined it to talk about a damn movie.

[]: Post by Burger of Power » Fri Oct 25, 2013 4:04 pm He's still being honest though. This place is for discussing, talking and various shenanigans regarding Eva. A possible delay of 4.0 is a subject that attains this place, unlike the humanitarian loss in Japan. The only exception would be if Gainax and any of its staff had happened to be among the victims. It's not that the victims do not matter, but it's perfectly right to worry over the future of Rebuild of Evangelion over them here.

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Fri Oct 25, 2013 4:07 pm Well, from what the article says, so far the situation seems under control, so I don't think we have to worry much about a delay. Besides it's not as if Japan didn't get earthquakes often!

[]: Post by Burger of Power » Fri Oct 25, 2013 4:09 pm Also, it looks pretty bad if you ask me. However, they are surely perfectly prepared at Gainax for this situations at this point... What does worry me is the super storm heading towards Japan even as we speak. That could get VERY ugly. EDIT: "Under control"... How many times have we heard those words only for them to be untrue?

[]: Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Oct 25, 2013 4:23 pm Gainax has virtually nothing to do with Eva anymore. It's all khara.

[]: Post by Xard » Sat Oct 26, 2013 6:40 am Merkaba wrote: I didn't join this forum to feign pity over the loss of human life. I joined it to talk about a damn movie.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Sat Oct 26, 2013 7:00 am Merkaba wrote: I didn't join this forum to feign pity over the loss of human life. I joined it to talk about a damn movie.

[]: Post by hui43210 » Sat Oct 26, 2013 8:53 am No tsunami, doesn't look like it'll cause too many problems. Japan is built to withstand earthquakes, tsunamis cause the real damage.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Oct 26, 2013 11:09 am Either it causes setbacks or it doesn't. We won't know, if at all, for years. Only thing this thread accomplishes is the tasteless trivialization of a natural disaster. No thanks.
